title: Problem Statement
definition: A concise description of a user segment's struggle, root cause, and negative outcome — without prescribing a solution.
whenToUse: At the start of any discovery initiative to align the team on what you are solving.
frameworkSteps: Describe the job or task they struggle with
interviewPrompts: Walk me through the last time you faced this problem.
What workarounds do you use today?
officeUse: Kick off discovery briefs, PRDs, and stakeholder alignment docs.

title: Assumption Mapping
definition: A technique to visualize and prioritize beliefs about users, problems, and solutions by importance and uncertainty.
whenToUse: When you have many unknowns and need to decide what to validate first.
frameworkSteps: List all assumptions about users, problem, and solution
interviewPrompts: What would have to be true for this product to succeed?
Which of these beliefs do we have the least evidence for?
officeUse: Discovery planning sessions and sprint zero workshops.

title: Problem-Solution Fit
definition: The stage where you have evidence that a real problem exists and your proposed solution addresses it meaningfully.
whenToUse: Before investing in building an MVP or scaling a feature.
frameworkSteps: Validate problem exists with user evidence
Confirm pain is frequent and severe
Test solution concept with target users
Measure willingness to adopt or pay
interviewPrompts: How do you solve this today?
What would make you switch to a new solution?

title: Discovery Brief
definition: A short document aligning the team on discovery goals, assumptions, research plan, and success criteria.
whenToUse: Starting any discovery initiative with cross-functional stakeholders.
frameworkSteps: State problem hypothesis and target user
officeUse: Share with engineering, design, and leadership before research sprints.

title: Interview Guide
definition: A structured script for user interviews with open-ended questions, probes, and success criteria.
whenToUse: Before conducting any user discovery interview.
frameworkSteps: Draft core exploration questions (past behavior, not hypotheticals)
interviewPrompts: Tell me about the last time you...
officeUse: Standardize interview quality across PM and UX team members.
